summary: Pre-prod release audit: 8 parallel specialists over teru/teruwm

result.core:
Audit complete. Findings below are from reading all of `src/core/` + `src/pty/` (and spot-checking `src/persist/Scrollback.zig`, `src/render/FontAtlas.zig`, call sites in `src/modes/`, `src/compositor/`).

# Core terminal correctness — release-blocker triage

## Findings

**HIGH | src/core/VtParser.zig:599-628 (also 564-595) | Colon (`:`) in CSI params aborts the sequence to ground; the tail renders as literal text** | `handleCsiParam`'s `else => self.state = .ground` fires on `0x3A`. Feeding `ESC[4:3m` (colon-form SGR: undercurl, underline-color `58:2::r:g:b` — emitted by neovim/kitty-protocol-aware apps) parses `4`, hits `:`, drops to ground, and then **prints `3m` into the grid as text**. Violates the repo's own rule ("every state transition must be exhaustive — no catch-all that silently drops bytes") and worse: it dumps sequence bytes into the pane. Default demo apps (fish, htop, claude-code/chalk) use semicolon form, but any user with neovim undercurl/underline-color will see garbage mid-demo. | Fix: S — treat `:` (and 0x3C-0x3F) as param bytes (collect-and-ignore subparams) so the sequence consumes through its final byte.

**HIGH | src/core/Grid.zig:597-660 (`scrollUpN`) | Scrollback capture is not gated on alt-screen or full-screen scroll region** | The push-to-scrollback only checks left/right margins (`is_margin_scroll`); it ignores (a) alt-screen active and (b) `scroll_top > 0` / `scroll_bottom < rows-1`. vim/less run on the alt screen with a DECSTBM region — every scroll inside them pushes the region's top line into the **main** scrollback. After quitting vim in a demo, scrolling up shows interleaved vim frames; `sb_lines` inflation also shifts Selection's absolute-row mapping while a TUI is scrolling. The comment at Grid.zig:604-605 claims "Only for full-width scrolls at screen top" but the code never checks "at screen top". xterm/alacritty/kitty suppress capture in both cases. | Fix: S/M — add a `Grid` flag set by `switchToAltScreen`/`switchToMainScreen`, and require `scroll_top == 0 and scroll_bottom == rows-1` before pushing.

**MED | src/core/VtParser.zig:1051-1119 (`dispatchCsiPrivate`) | DECSET/DECRST handle only `params[0]`** | `ESC[?1000;1006h` (combined mouse-mode enable — emitted by several TUIs and tmux) enables only mode 1000; SGR-mouse 1006 is silently dropped → mouse clicks report legacy X10 encoding and break >223-column panes. Loop over `0..param_count` is the fix. Also DECRQM (line 1148-1164) reports "not recognized" (0) for modes teru does support (7/12/69). | Fix: S.

**MED | src/core/VtParser.zig:880-920 ('A'/'B'/'E'/'F') | Cursor-up/down clamp to the scroll region even when the cursor starts outside it — moving the cursor the wrong direction** | CUU at row 0 with `scroll_top=5` forces the cursor **down** to row 5; CUD on a status line below `scroll_bottom` yanks it **up** to the region bottom. Per VT510/xterm, margins only bound movement when the cursor starts inside the region. Apps that park a status line outside the DECSTBM region (vim cmdline-style layouts) can get rows painted at wrong positions. | Fix: S — only clamp to the margin if the cursor started at-or-inside it.

**MED | src/core/Grid.zig:567-573 (`cursorDown`) + VtParser.zig:494-500 (IND) | LF/IND with cursor below the scroll region scrolls the region instead of moving down** | `cursor_row >= scroll_bottom` is true for any row *below* the region too, so output below the region spuriously scrolls region content (and the cursor never advances). Correct: scroll only when `cursor_row == scroll_bottom`; below the region, move down until screen bottom. Same off-by-class as the finding above; together they're the main "TUI with a region garbles" risk. | Fix: S.

**MED | src/core/Grid.zig:597/705 (`scrollUpN`/`scrollDownN`), VtParser.zig:978-984 (REP) | Unclamped `n` from hostile/garbled input: `ESC[65535S` does 65535 full-region memcpys + scrollback pushes** | CSI params saturate at u16 max; SU/SD loop `n` times (each iteration: ~rows×cols×16B memcpy + SGR-encoding a 4 KiB line + heap-duped scrollback delta + periodic full-grid keyframe via `Scrollback.pushLine`). One sequence ≈ GBs of memory traffic and wipes the user's real history with 65 K junk lines; `cat /dev/urandom` or a malicious payload stalls the pane and floods scrollback. IL/DL/ICH/DCH/ECH clamp correctly; SU/SD/REP do not. | Fix: S — clamp SU/SD to region height (scroll ≥ height == clear region), clamp REP to `cols` (xterm behavior).

**MED | src/core/Grid.zig:811-865 (`resize`) | Error-path leaves Grid inconsistent: OOM between cell swap and `rows/cols` update, and alt-buffer UAF** | Order is: swap in `new_cells` (old freed) → `try alloc(RowMeta)` → set `rows/cols`. If the meta alloc fails, `cells.len` no longer matches `rows*cols`; `Multiplexer.resizePanePtys` (Multiplexer.zig:491) catches the error and **continues**, so the next `vt.feed` indexes out of bounds → panic. Also lines 854-859: `allocator.free(old_alt)` happens **before** `try alloc` — on failure `self.alt_cells` dangles → use-after-free on the next `ESC[?1049h`. OOM-only, but it converts "allocation failed" into "crash a live pane". | Fix: S — allocate all three buffers up-front before mutating any state.

**MED/LOW | src/core/VtParser.zig:407-451 (`handleUtf8`/`decodeUtf8`) | No UTF-8 validity checks: overlong encodings, surrogates, and >U+10FFFF reach the grid** | Overlong NUL (`C0 80`) decodes to 0 and `grid.write(0)` plants a cell whose `char==0` **is the wide-trailer sentinel** — renderer/clipboard/scrollback all skip it, producing a phantom invisible column and column drift in scrollback re-encode. Overlong C0 (`C1 9B` → 0x5B) renders control-range codepoints; surrogates/0x110000-0x1FFFFF land in cells (FontAtlas lookups are range-guarded, so no OOB — display only). Also a lead byte interrupted by ASCII leaves stale `utf8_len`, so a later stray continuation glues to the stale buffer. | Fix: S — validate in `decodeUtf8` (overlong/surrogate/range → 0xFFFD) and reset `utf8_len` when a non-continuation arrives mid-sequence.

**LOW | src/pty/PosixPty.zig:110-112 | Unbounded `@memcpy(shell_buf[0..shell.len], shell)` from `$SHELL`** | `shell_buf` is `max_path_bytes`; a longer `$SHELL` panics (safe builds) / smashes the child stack (ReleaseFast). Child-side only → dead pane, not a crash of teru. The `cwd` copy 4 lines above does check length; mirror it. | Fix: S.

**LOW | src/core/VtParser.zig:132-141 (`sendResponse`) | Partial/failed `std.c.write` of DA1/DSR/DECRQM responses silently dropped** | On a full O_NONBLOCK PTY buffer, a truncated `ESC[24;80R` leaves the querying app parsing a half-response. Rare, but a stuck `vim` waiting on DSR during heavy output is demo-visible. | Fix: S/M — small retry loop like Clipboard.writeAllToPty, or queue.

**LOW | src/core/VtParser.zig:750-761 (OSC 8) | `hyperlink_next_id` wraps 255→1 and overwrites slots still referenced by on-screen cells** | After 255 links, old cells' `hyperlink_id` silently points at a new URI — clicking opens the wrong (potentially attacker-chosen) URL. The struct comment ("slots are reused when a link is closed") describes logic that doesn't exist. | Fix: M (refcount or clear cells on reuse); acceptable to ship with a note.

**LOW | src/core/Multiplexer.zig:527 (`pollPtys`) | `readAndProcess(buf) catch 0` swallows real PTY errors with no log** | Violates repo anti-pattern rule #6 ("DON'T add catch {} silently"). EIO on child death is masked until the liveness sweep; any other error class is invisible forever. Same pattern at Terminal.zig:161 (unchecked partial stdout write in `--raw` bridge → dropped output bytes under backpressure). | Fix: S.

**LOW | dead code | `VtParser.setGrid`/`setAllocator` (VtParser.zig:149-155) have zero call sites (linkVt assigns fields directly); `Multiplexer.scrollback` field (Multiplexer.zig:30,186,197) is never set non-null; `ViMode.getViewportSelection` (ViMode.zig:409) has no callers** | Dead pane-state plumbing invites drift (e.g. someone "fixing" setGrid instead of linkVt, missing the response_fd/scrollback re-link). | Fix: S — delete.

**INFO | gaps, not regressions** | DECOM (mode 6), DECCOLM (mode 3), HTS/TBC (custom tab stops), CBT (`CSI Z`) unimplemented — fish/htop/vim/claude-code don't need them. DECSET 47/1047/1049 all treated as 1049 (minor). OSC 4 palette queries unanswered (apps time out gracefully). The documented invariants I could falsify all held: CSI 16-param cap, OSC 256 truncation, DCS `.dcs_st_esc` isolation (with regression tests), zero-dim grid guard at feed(), pane re-link discipline on append/orderedRemove (correct and well-commented), Clipboard paste sanitisation (genuinely thorough: marker-escape, binary detection, 201~ recovery).

## Suggested pre-demo priorities
1. CSI-colon fix (S) and SU/SD/REP clamp (S) — both tiny, both close real "garbage on screen" doors.
2. Alt-screen/region scrollback gate (S/M) — the most likely *visible* embarrassment with vim/less.
3. Region-clamp direction fixes (S) — low risk, spec-aligning.
The rest can ride a 0.8.4 patch.

## Summary
Core is in genuinely good shape for a pre-1.0 terminal: bounds discipline on untrusted PTY input is consistently careful (clamped narrowing casts, saturating arithmetic, guarded buffers), the historical crash classes (DCS leak, zero-dim grid, pane move/re-link) are fixed with regression tests, and Clipboard/Selection are defensively written. The two real demo risks are text-garbling, not crashing: colon-form CSI dumping sequence tails as literal text, and alt-screen/region scrolls polluting main scrollback. No release blocker found; ship after the three S-sized fixes above, with the OOM-ordering resize fix queued next.
result.compositor:
COMPOSITOR STABILITY AUDIT — teru @ feat/teruwm-daily-driver, src/compositor/ (all 13 listed modules read in full; tiling/Workspace.zig, vendor glue spot-checked)

FINDINGS

BLOCKER | src/compositor/ServerFocus.zig:117-128 + ServerWindow.zig:346-403 + ServerCursor.zig:537-578 | `Server.drag_terminal` is never cleared when a pane dies — `clearFocusRefs` nulls `focused_terminal`/`focused_view`/`grab_node_id` only, and neither `closeNode` nor `handleTerminalExit` touches `drag_terminal`. Confirmed by grep: the only writer that nulls it is `terminalMouseRelease` (button-up). Meanwhile `tickDragAutoScroll` dereferences `server.drag_terminal` (`tp.mouse`, `tp.renderer`, `tp.pane`) on EVERY frame callback (Output.zig:157), and `terminalMouseMotion` on every pointer motion. | Hold left-button drag-select over a pane while its shell exits, or while an MCP agent (the product's headline feature) closes that pane → freed `*TerminalPane` deref at vsync rate → SIGSEGV mid-demo. This is exactly the grab-on-close class CLAUDE.md warns about, applied to the fourth, forgotten pointer. | Fix: S — in `clearFocusRefs`, add `if (server.drag_terminal) |d| if (d.node_id == node_id) { server.drag_terminal = null; }` (both close paths already route through it).

HIGH | src/compositor/Server.zig:1315-1344 (`spawnTerminal`) | Violates the documented one-of-three focus invariant (Server.zig:164-166) three ways: (1) `focused_xwayland` is neither deactivated nor nulled; (2) previous `focused_view` is nulled without `wlr_xdg_toplevel_set_activated(prev, false)`; (3) `ws.setFocus(tp.node_id)` is never called, so `Workspace.active_node` stays on the OLD pane (`Workspace.addNode` at tiling/Workspace.zig:116 doesn't set focus). | (1) `closeFocused` (ServerWindow.zig:322) checks `focused_xwayland` BEFORE `focused_terminal` → with an X11 app (Emacs/GIMP) focused, Mod+Return then Mod+X closes the X11 app instead of the new terminal, on stage. (2) browser keeps "active" chrome → two windows look focused. (3) any later `updateFocusedTerminal` (workspace round-trip, scratchpad hide) bounces focus back to the previous pane — the A1 three-authorities drift, live. | Fix: S — route spawnTerminal's focus through the same normalize used by `focusTerminalByNode` (deactivate both prevs, null all three, `ws.setFocus`). Directly feeds pending task #11.

HIGH | src/compositor/ServerRestart.zig:340-343 (`restoreSession`) | After restoring panes, only `setWorkspaceVisibility(active_ws, true)` + `arrangeworkspace(active_ws)` run. Panes restored onto OTHER workspaces are created with scene nodes enabled (TerminalPane.createRestored never disables) and are never positioned or hidden — no `recomputeVisibility()` call anywhere in the restore path (grep confirms). | Hot-restart (Mod+', a demo centerpiece) with populated side workspaces composites foreign panes at (0,0) ON TOP of the live workspace — not "briefly" as task #35 says, but until the first workspace switch. Visually broken restart in front of an audience. | Fix: S — call `server.recomputeVisibility()` after the restore loop (R3 already derives hidden for workspaces no output shows). Upgrade task #35's severity.

HIGH | src/compositor/XdgView.zig:99-166 + XwaylandView.zig:100-130 (`handleMap`) | A window matching a `[rules]` workspace assignment is registered on `ws`, arranged for `ws`'s geometry — but its scene node is never disabled (no `recomputeVisibility` in either map path) and `focusView(view)` / `focusXwaylandSurface` runs unconditionally. | Launch an app rule-assigned to a background workspace while presenting on ws 1: the window renders mispositioned over the current workspace AND steals keyboard focus into an off-workspace surface — typing vanishes. Only paths that call `recomputeVisibility` (focusWorkspace/moveNode) repair it. | Fix: S — after registration: `recomputeVisibility()`; skip the focus call when `outputShowing(ws) == null`.

MED | src/compositor/XdgView.zig:212-260 (`handleUnmap`) | Closing a Wayland client (its own UI, or Mod+X → send_close → unmap) clears focus refs and re-tiles but never re-derives focus — no `updateFocusedTerminal()`, unlike `handleTerminalExit` (ServerWindow.zig:394-401, which even re-seats `active_node` on a survivor). The defensive warn at ServerWindow.zig:334-343 ("closeFocused with no focus") is telemetry for this exact hole. | Demo flow "close the browser" leaves keyboard focus null: typing dead, Mod+X inert, until a click/Mod+J. Looks like a hang on stage. | Fix: S — mirror handleTerminalExit's survivor re-seat + `updateFocusedTerminal()` at the end of unmap (and the destroy-without-unmap path).

MED | src/compositor/ServerCursor.zig:309-330 (`tryBeginBorderDrag`) | Iterates ALL `terminal_panes` — including panes on other workspaces and PARKED scratchpads, whose `pos_x/width` are stale — and tests only X (no Y, no visibility, no floating check). Defaults (WmConfig.zig:242-243) make a 10px full-height strip per matching edge where every non-Super left-press is swallowed into `.border_drag` (master_ratio jumps if the mouse moves). | A hidden scratchpad (35-40% wide, centered — demo uses scratchpads) leaves a dead-click strip near screen center; panes on background workspaces add more. Symptom: "clicks randomly don't register / layout twitches" — unexplainable live. | Fix: S — filter to `workspace[slot] == active_workspace and !floating[slot]` and require ≥2 tiled nodes containing that edge.

MED | src/compositor/ServerWindow.zig:294-310 (`closeNode`) | For XDG views, only `focused_view` is matched; closing any UNFOCUSED Wayland window by node_id returns false — despite `nodes.xdg_view[slot]` holding the exact back-pointer needed (Node.zig:73). | The MCP `teruwm_close_window` path (agents managing windows is the product pitch) silently no-ops on unfocused browsers. An on-stage "Claude, close the browser" fails if focus moved. | Fix: S — resolve `slot = nodes.findById(node_id)`, cast `xdg_view[slot]`, `send_close`; same for `xwayland_surface[slot]`.

MED | src/compositor/ServerCursor.zig:343-389 + 421-423 (`forwardAndFocus`/`syncWsActiveIndex`) | Click-focus always writes `setFocus(nid)` into `workspaces[layout_engine.active_workspace]` and never updates `focused_output`. On 2 outputs (laptop + projector), clicking a window on the non-focused output stores a foreign node_id as the focused output's workspace `active_node`. | Next `updateFocusedTerminal` (ServerFocus.zig:307-309) resolves that foreign id and focuses a pane on the other monitor — keyboard goes to the wrong screen during a multi-output presentation. | Fix: M — derive the output from cursor position (`wlr_output_layout_output_at`), set `focused_output`, and sync the node's own workspace.

LOW (known class — instances only, task #36 filed) | src/compositor/Server.zig:946-961 + 1002-1018 | `deinit` runs after `wl_display_destroy` (main.zig defer order) yet removes timer sources (`keybind_repeat_src`, `bar_tick_src`, `terminal_repeat_src`), calls `bar.deinitExecs()` (Bar.zig:462 → `wl_event_source_remove`), and `safeRemoveListener`s 17 listeners whose signals live inside display-owned objects (xdg_shell, seat, managers) already freed — UAF writes on every clean shutdown. Listed for completeness; matches the filed class, no NEW instances found beyond these.

LOW | src/compositor/XdgView.zig:57-87 | `create`: if `miozu_xdg_surface_surface` returns null (line 77) the allocated view leaks with map/unmap/commit listeners already... actually registration happens after — pure struct leak. Cosmetic.

LOW | src/compositor/ServerScratchpad.zig:291-297 (`spawn`) | On `addTerminal` failure (registry full) the pane is deinit'd but its `pane_index` entry (registered in `initWithSpawn`, TerminalPane.zig:163) is not removed → `terminalPaneById` returns a freed pointer afterward. 256-node-full only; same hazard if `pane_index.put` ever OOMs (silent `catch {}`). Fix: S — `_ = server.pane_index.remove(tp.node_id)` on that path.

LOW | src/compositor/ServerFocus.zig:167-172 (`cycleFocusAll`) | `current_id` anchor ignores `focused_xwayland` → Win+J from an X11 window restarts the ring at the stale tiled anchor instead of the neighbour. Annoyance, not a crash.

WHAT I WOULD NOT DO LIVE ON STAGE: (1) drag-select text while an agent is closing panes via MCP (blocker above); (2) hot-restart with anything open on background workspaces — or if you must, immediately Mod+N round-trip to repair visibility; (3) Mod+X right after spawning a terminal over an X11 app; (4) close the browser with Mod+X and immediately keep typing — click a pane first; (5) extend to a second output and click-focus across monitors; (6) ask the agent to close an unfocused window by id.

SUMMARY
The post-v0.4.25 defensive set genuinely covers the historical coredump classes (surface liveness, set-cursor filter, seat teardown ordering, restore clamps are all in place and well-commented), but one stale-pointer of the same shape slipped through: `drag_terminal` survives pane death and is dereferenced every frame — the single pre-launch must-fix. The remaining risk is concentrated in focus-authority drift (spawnTerminal bypasses all normalization; map/unmap paths skip visibility/focus re-derivation), which produces wrong-window closes and dead-keyboard moments rather than crashes — embarrassing but recoverable on stage. Overall: crash-hardened core with ~6 small (S-sized) correctness patches needed tonight; nothing found that requires architectural work before tomorrow.
result.perf:
All evidence gathered. Findings below.

---

## Performance + resource hygiene audit — teruwm/teru render path (branch feat/teruwm-daily-driver, v0.8.3)

### Verification of known open items

**1. Sync MCP screenshot blocks loop — STILL TRUE, worse on the keybind path**
HIGH | `src/compositor/WmMcpServer.zig:306-315` → `src/compositor/ServerScreenshot.zig:96-119` | `handleRequestFd` dispatches tools synchronously on the wl event loop. `compositeAndWrite` re-renders every visible pane (`tp.render()` = full SIMD repaint each), allocates a full-output u32 buffer, and `png.write` emits *uncompressed* PNG ("~6MB for 1920x1080" per `src/png.zig:5` — ~14MB at 2560×1440) with bytewise CRC32+Adler and synchronous `fwrite`. The Mod+W path (`takeScreenshot`, ServerScreenshot.zig:46-58) is worse: encodes/writes the PNG **twice** (dated + `latest.png`), then `copyToClipboard` reads the file back. For an AI-first compositor where the agent's "eyes" are screenshots, every MCP capture freezes input+vsync 100-400ms. Demo-visible hitch. Fix: thread or at least defer encode to idle callback; skip `latest.png` on the MCP path. Size M. (Task #6 still valid.)

**2. Unbounded PTY drain — PARTLY MITIGATED, downgrade**
MED | `src/compositor/TerminalPane.zig:313-333` | `poll()` now caps at 4×8KB per dispatch, and `Output.handleFrame` gates fallback polls (`frames_since_pty_input`, Output.zig:181-198). However a flooding pane re-fires its level-triggered fd every dispatch cycle — no aggregate per-frame byte budget, so `yes`-style floods still saturate the loop with parse work between input events. Input is serviced but latency grows under load. Fix: per-frame global drain budget or defer reads once grid is already dirty. Size S-M. (Task #7: keep, lower priority.)

**3. Full-buffer GLES re-upload per frame — STILL TRUE, intentional**
HIGH | `src/compositor/TerminalPane.zig:386-391, 449-460` | `dirty_y0/dirty_y1 = -1` sentinel forces full-buffer damage on every commit (glue `miozu_scene_buffer_commit_dirty`, vendor/miozu-wlr-glue.c:1189-1220, confirms full-region fallback). Comment documents the gles2 partial-upload corruption that forced the revert of 738d0d4. CPU render is still dirty-row-only; the GPU re-uploads the whole pane (~14MB/frame at 2560×1376) every dirty frame. This is the prime suspect for low-fps-low-CPU scroll (DMA-bound, paces frame completion). Fix = double-buffered wlr_buffers with partial damage. Size L — not for tomorrow; document as accepted. (Tasks #8/#31.)

**4. ~1MB/pane leak — ROOT CAUSE FOUND (new, different from the scene-node theory)**
HIGH | `src/compositor/TerminalPane.zig:109-120` (also 221-230 `createRestored`; `src/compositor/Bar.zig:143-147`) | `SoftwareRenderer.initWithScheme` heap-allocates a `pixel_w×pixel_h` u32 framebuffer (software.zig:135-137); `initWithSpawn` then re-points `renderer.framebuffer` at the wlr pixel buffer's data **without freeing the original allocation**, and `TerminalPane.deinit` never calls `renderer.deinit()`. At the 24×80 spawn size that's ~1-1.7MB orphaned **per pane spawn** — matching the measured "~1MB/pane". Bar leaks 2×~250KB once. Meanwhile the deferred scene-destroy queue (#11) **is implemented** (`Server.zig:897-936` `queueSceneDestroy`/`drainSceneDestroy`, wired in `TerminalPane.deinit:773-787`) — needs the live verify task #5 notes. Fix: `allocator.free(renderer.framebuffer)` before adopting the wlr data pointer (3 call sites). Size S. Ship-tomorrow candidate.

**5. Scroll ~25fps on 165Hz, low CPU — pacing path is clean; two concrete explanations**
MED | `src/compositor/ServerCursor.zig:96-129` | Axis handler correctly coalesces (markAllDirty + scheduleRender, render once per vsync). But frames are only scheduled when an axis event arrives — for a **discrete wheel, scroll fps == notch rate (~25Hz)**: that is by design, not a bug; smoothness would need pixel-interpolated animation frames. For touchpad/fast rates, the cap is then #3's full upload (commit-time). `teruwm_perf` now exposes `frame_interval_sum_us` + `commit_time_sum_us` (WmMcpTools.zig:752-766) — run it live while scrolling to split the two before burning more time. (Task #31 is executable now.)

### New findings

**6. Hidden-workspace busy panes are fully rendered every frame**
HIGH | `src/compositor/Output.zig:184-197` + `src/compositor/TerminalPane.zig:734-747` | `handleFrame` loops **all** `terminal_panes` and calls `renderIfDirty()` with no visibility check; `ptyReadable` schedules frames on **every output** whenever any pane has data. A build/log/Claude-stream pane parked on a hidden workspace burns a full SIMD repaint + buffer commit per vsync and keeps all outputs awake indefinitely. For the target audience (many agent panes on background workspaces) this is the biggest steady-state CPU/power item after #3. Fix: skip render (leave grid dirty) when the pane's node is disabled/parked/on a non-shown workspace; render-on-reveal in `recomputeVisibility`; gate `ptyReadable`'s schedule on visibility. Size M.

**7. Font-atlas zoom: 1384-glyph synchronous re-raster per wheel notch (3× worse after 456→1384 growth)**
MED | `src/compositor/ServerCursor.zig:81-91` → `TerminalPane.zig:562-595` → `src/render/FontAtlas.zig:468-642` | Alt+scroll calls `zoomFont` inline in the axis handler: each notch re-rasterizes all 1384 extended-budget glyphs via stbtt (`rasterizeAtSize`), plus **copies the main + fallback font files** (FontAtlas.zig:609-619, ~0.5-4MB memcpy+alloc per notch), then full pane re-grid + repaint. Estimate ~10-40ms/notch ReleaseFast, 5-10× that in debug builds (`make compositor` is debug — don't demo on a debug binary). A fast 8-notch burst = sequential re-rasters of intermediate sizes that are immediately discarded. Startup cost (main + up to 3 variants = 4×1384 glyphs ≈ 40-160ms release) is acceptable one-time. Fix: defer zoom to the frame callback and collapse bursts to the final size (mirror the `resize_pending_id` pattern); optionally share `font_data` via refcount instead of copying. Size S-M.

**8. Stale glyph-count comment will mislead future sizing**
LOW | `src/render/FontAtlas.zig:282` | Comment says "Totals: 360 (ascii) / 456 (extended) / 968 (full)" while the authoritative `atlas_total_glyphs` (line 73-74) is 360/1384/1640. One-line doc fix. Size S.

**9. Verified-clean (no action): hot paths hold the zero-alloc rule.** `renderRangeSel`/`blitGlyphRow` (software.zig) allocate nothing; fillRect/blit are branch-light SIMD; `glyphSlot` is O(1) compares. `Bar.render` per frame is signature-gated (Bar.zig:215-264), `refreshCachedData` is TTL-gated (BarRenderer.zig:147-205), exec widgets are non-blocking with correct fd cleanup (zombie leak #9 fixed). Layout scratch uses a 16KB FBA (Server.zig:220). Per-frame syscalls are clock_gettime (vDSO) only. Drag-select dirty-row tracking is properly minimal (ServerCursor.zig:476-520). `barSignature`'s `countInWorkspace` is an O(≤256) hash-iter per frame — negligible.

### Suggested release-blocker triage
- Fix before demo (S): #4 framebuffer-adoption leak; #8 comment.
- Fix if time (S-M): #7 zoom debounce; #1 skip `latest.png` double-encode on MCP path.
- Document as known, fix in v0.8.4: #1 full async screenshot, #3 partial damage double-buffering, #6 hidden-pane render skip, #2 drain budget.

---

**Summary:** The per-frame render core is genuinely clean (zero-alloc SIMD, dirty-row tracking, signature-gated bar, coalesced scroll) — the architecture is sound for the demo. The remaining costs are concentrated in four places: synchronous PNG screenshots on the event loop, full-buffer GPU re-upload (accepted GLES workaround, likely the scroll-fps cap), hidden-pane rendering with no visibility gate, and a newly root-caused ~1MB-per-spawn orphaned framebuffer that is an S-size fix. Nothing found is a hard blocker; #4 (leak, trivial) and #7 (zoom burst lag, demo-visible if anyone Alt+scrolls) are the highest-value fixes achievable before tomorrow.

## MCP Surface + Security Audit — teru/teruwm (release-blocker triage)

I read the full MCP path on both surfaces: `McpServer.zig`, `McpDispatch.zig`, `McpServerTools.zig`, `McpFramework.zig`, `McpTools.zig`, `in_band.zig`, `protocol.zig`, `forward.zig`, `HookHandler/Listener`, `ipc.zig`, `compat.zig`, `png.zig`, and the compositor `WmMcpServer.zig` / `WmMcpTools.zig` / `Session.zig` / `Bar.zig` / `BarWidget.zig`. Findings, most severe first.

---

**BLOCKER | src/compositor/WmMcpTools.zig:70,76,84,95,102,240,254,267,272,274,285,311 (+ Makefile:51) | One malformed MCP int crashes the entire Wayland session.**
The shipped release is **ReleaseSafe** (`make release` → `zig build -Doptimize=ReleaseSafe`), where an out-of-range `@intCast` is checked illegal behavior → panic → process abort. Every compositor thunk pulls untrusted JSON ints through `extractNestedJsonIntSigned` (i64, may be negative or huge) and `@intCast`s them to narrow types **before** the range check, which lives inside the tool body. Examples that abort teruwm — killing every running Wayland client and all unsaved work:
- `teruwm_switch_workspace {"workspace":999}` → `@intCast(i64→u8)` panic (the `ws>=10` guard is inside `toolSwitchWorkspace`, after the cast at :95).
- `teruwm_close_window {"node_id":-1}` → `@intCast(i64→u64)` panic (:70).
- `teruwm_move_to_workspace {"workspace":300}` (:84); `teruwm_click {"x":9999999999}` → `@intCast(i64→i32)` (:285); `teruwm_mouse_path {"duration_ms":99999999999}` (`@intCast(@max(0,d))` still overflows u32, :267).
Why it matters: the headline feature is "AI controls your WM." An LLM picking `workspace: 99`, or any same-uid process touching the documented socket, hard-crashes the user's whole desktop — a realistic *accidental* trigger during tomorrow's presentation, not just an adversarial one. The team already knows this class (teru side added `clampU32` at McpServerTools.zig:50 "so an oversized count param can't trip an illegal-narrowing @intCast panic in the shipped ReleaseSafe build") but never applied it to the compositor thunks. **Fix size: S** — validate/clamp every int before the cast (range-check `node_id≥0`, `0≤workspace<10`, clamp coords) in each thunk.

**HIGH | src/agent/McpFramework.zig:295-312 + src/compositor/WmMcpServer.zig:297-304 | `TERU_MCP_READONLY` does not cover the compositor — read-only is a false promise.**
`handleToolsCall` enforces read-only only against teru's own `write_tool_names`; any `teruwm_*` tool isn't in that list and isn't in teru's `tool_table`, so it falls straight through to the `forward` branch (:307-312) and is proxied to the compositor. The teruwm server's `framework_config` has **no `read_only` block at all**. So a deployment that sets `TERU_MCP_READONLY=1` still exposes `teruwm_quit`, `teruwm_restart`, `teruwm_spawn_terminal`, and the AI-input tools `teruwm_type`/`teruwm_press`/`teruwm_click` (arbitrary keystrokes/commands into any focused client), plus `teruwm_set_config`. Security-conscious users will reasonably believe read-only mode neutralizes the attack surface; it does the opposite of what they expect. **Fix size: M** — add a `read_only` allowlist to `WmMcpServer` and/or make teru's forward reject mutating `teruwm_*` when read-only is active.

**HIGH | src/modes/windowed.zig:1272 (and :1295-1301) | OSC 9999 answerback injection from untrusted pane content.**
OSC 9999 sequences are parsed from *every* pane's PTY output regardless of which program emitted them. `agent:start` runs `hooks.fire(.agent_start)` **unconditionally** (not gated by `agent_in_band`), which `forkExec`s `/bin/sh -c <user's on_agent_start cmd>` (Hooks.zig:62-75). So any content displayed in a pane — `cat` a file, `curl` output, a tailed log, `ls` of a directory with a crafted filename — can forge process-graph agents, relocate panes via `autoAssignAgentWorkspace`, and trigger the user's hook command. The `.query` → `in_band.handleQuery` path (:1295) is gated by `agent_in_band` (default false, good) and forces `pane_id` to the emitting pane (in_band.zig:106-127, solid), but when a user enables it, untrusted content can drive allowlisted reads + `teru_set_widget` → **status-bar spoofing** (fake "VPN connected", fake notifications). This is the classic terminal-answerback CVE class. **Fix size: M** — gate *all* OSC 9999 agent control behind `agent_in_band` (not just `.query`), and document the risk.

**MED | src/compat.zig:275-290 | Screenshot path allowlist too broad + prefix-match bug → arbitrary $HOME file clobber.**
`isSafeScreenshotPath` permits any path under `$HOME` and uses `startsWith(path, home)` with no trailing-slash boundary — `HOME=/home/ng` also matches `/home/ng-evil/…`. `png.zig` opens with `O_NOFOLLOW` (blocks symlink swaps) but that does **not** stop truncating an existing regular file. `teruwm_screenshot`/`teru_screenshot`/`teruwm_screenshot_pane` can therefore overwrite `~/.bashrc`, `~/.config/teru/teru.conf`, `~/.ssh/*` with PNG bytes. Not RCE (content is uncontrolled framebuffer bytes) but a real corruption/DoS the user won't expect from a "screenshot" tool. **Fix size: S** — restrict to a screenshots dir (or `/tmp` only), and fix the prefix check to require a `home + "/"` boundary.

**MED | architectural (WmMcpServer + WmMcpTools.zig:455, ServerConfig/Bar.zig:355, BarWidget.zig:152) | The "no `teruwm_exec`" rule is satisfied only in letter — the MCP socket is shell-equivalent.**
`teruwm_spawn_terminal` (spawns the user shell) + `teruwm_type` + `teruwm_press Return` = arbitrary command execution as the user. Separately, `teruwm_reload_config` re-reads `~/.config/teruwm/config`, which supports `{exec:N:<sh command>}` bar widgets executed via `execve("/bin/sh","-c",…)` (Bar.zig:355; parser BarWidget.zig:152). The only real boundary is the uid-scoped socket (`chmod 0600` + `SO_PEERCRED`, ipc.zig:185/207-213; root always allowed, documented). So the true security model is "any process running as your UID can run commands and drive your WM," and there is **no consent, confirmation, or audit log** on destructive tools (`quit`, `restart`, `type`, `click`). This needs to be stated plainly to the security-conscious audience rather than implied away by the absence of an `exec` tool. **Fix size: M** (docs + optional: a kill-switch env to disable input-synthesis tools / a confirmation gate).

**LOW | src/agent/McpServerTools.zig:660 | `teru_set_config` value is a config-injection near-miss.**
The line `"{key} = {value}\n"` writes `value` verbatim to teru.conf; `key` is allowlisted but `value` is not validated for control characters. It's safe *today* only by two accidents: line_json framing truncates the request at the first real `\n` (McpFramework.zig:204), and the value is **not** run through `unescapeJson` (so `\n` stays two literal chars). If a future change mirrors `send_input`'s `unescapeJson` onto the value, this becomes config-injection → RCE via an injected `{exec:}` widget on the hot-reload that `set_config` itself triggers. **Fix size: S** — explicitly reject newlines/control chars in `value`.

**LOW | src/server/ipc.zig:178-185 | chmod-after-bind race; relies entirely on SO_PEERCRED.**
The socket is created by `bind()` then `chmod(0600)` — a brief window at umask perms. `SO_PEERCRED` at accept (:207-213) closes the actual exposure, and `$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR` is mode 0700, so this is defense-in-depth only. Consider `umask(0077)` around the bind. (Also worth noting: on the macOS/`/tmp` fallback the socket sits in a world-traversable dir; peercred still guards it.) **Fix size: S.**

Things I checked that are **correctly handled**: session-name path traversal is blocked on both surfaces via `isSafeFilename` (rejects `/`, `\`, `..`, NUL — compat.zig:259; used by `getSessionPath` SessionDef.zig:409, and the compositor routes through it too); the in-band `pane_id` force/forged-key guard (`isPlainKey`, `overridePaneId`) is genuinely robust with good tests; `extractJsonString` correctly skips `\"` and drops control chars on the *escape* path; response buffers are bounded (`max_response` 65536, in-band 16K, event buffers truncate); teru-side write tools enforce read-only at the socket (defense vs bridge bypass); push widgets are text-only (no exec). The hand-rolled parser not unescaping `\uXXXX` is actually *protective* here — `/` can't be smuggled past `isSafeFilename`.

---

**3-line health summary:** The MCP authz model is thoughtfully built — uid-scoped sockets + SO_PEERCRED, in-band default-off with a well-tested pane-scoping guard, path-traversal sanitization on session names, O_NOFOLLOW screenshots — but it has one true release blocker: unvalidated `@intCast` of untrusted JSON ints in the compositor thunks panics the ReleaseSafe binary, so a single bad `workspace`/`node_id`/coordinate (easily an AI mistake) crashes the entire Wayland session. Two HIGH gaps undercut the security story: `TERU_MCP_READONLY` doesn't cover the compositor surface (where the dangerous input-synthesis tools live), and un-gated OSC 9999 `agent:start` lets untrusted pane content fire user hooks. The headline thesis ("AI controls your WM") is honest but the trust boundary — same-uid = full shell + WM control, no audit/consent — must be documented before this reaches security-conscious users tomorrow.

## Morning-of-demo smoke script

Create `tools/demo-smoke.sh` (~12 min total):
```
Phase A — headless, automated (~7 min, on the demo laptop):
  make test && make test-wm                       # inline suites
  make e2e-wm && make audit-wm                    # wired WM suites
  python3 tests/session_survival_e2e.py
  python3 tests/daemon_resize_stress.py
  python3 tests/many_pane_e2e.py
  env -u TERM_PROGRAM -u TERU_NESTED python3 tests/interactive_attach_e2e.py   # after the S-fix
  fail fast on any non-zero rc

Phase B — real hardware, manual checklist (~5 min, free VT via pkg/startt):
  1. startt → compositor up, bar renders, no dGPU wake (task #23 caveat)
  2. $mod+Enter ×3, $mod+Space cycle 2 layouts, $mod+1/2 + $mod+Escape
  3. $mod+T scratchpad on/off; launch the browser you'll demo (Wayland) + one X11 app (Xwayland)
  4. Ctrl+Shift+C a selection → paste into browser; $mod+W → toast + latest.png
  5. PLUG THE PROJECTOR NOW, not on stage: $mod+O, $mod+Shift+O, unplug, replug
  6. $mod+' hot-restart with everything open — panes + scratchpad survive, no (0,0) pile-up
  7. $mod+Shift+Q → VT must return to a usable console (f01dafd class)
  8. ssh localhost → teru -n demo → Ctrl+B d detaches, reattach resumes
```

## Feature-vs-test map (condensed)

| Feature area | Coverage |
|---|---|
| VT parse/grid/vi-mode/selection (model) | Strong — ~570 inline tests incl. 72 VtParser, 26 ViMode/Selection |
| Daemon survival / resize / 40-pane / wire cmds (0..21) | Strong — 4 passing E2Es + daemon_commands_e2e |
| TUI attach, click-focus, nested mode | Good test, currently hung by env leak (blocker fix S) |
| WM tiling/layouts/workspaces/keybind actions | Good — audit-wm + e2e_teruwm (once paths fixed) |
| MCP envelope/robustness (both servers, 22+38 tools) | Good but unwired (conformance/robustness/full-snapshots run manually only) |
| Scratchpads | OK headless; broken script path; restart interaction untested |
| Hot-restart, clipboard, scroll overlay, zoom_focused | Weak — manual-only or no assertion |
| Xwayland, multi-monitor, seat/VT, notifications-toast render | None |

## 3-line summary

Core terminal/daemon logic and the headless WM happy-path are genuinely well-tested and pass today on this machine; the verification *infrastructure* is the weak layer — CI/release have been dead since the Zig 0.17 migration, one standard suite deadlocks on the demo box itself, and the richest WM suite points at a nonexistent path. The riskiest demo moves (projector hotplug, X11 app, hot-restart with scratchpads, bare-TTY quit) are exactly the zero-coverage zones. Fix the two blockers + path rot tonight (all S-sized), run the Phase-B hardware checklist once before the talk, and the release is defensible; ship the hot-restart/dual-output/Xwayland E2Es before real users pile on.

Everything else in zig-terminal.md's API table I checked **is accurate against dev.813 source**: `Init{minimal,gpa,io}` (process.zig:31–44), `Args.Iterator.initAllocator` (process/Args.zig:46), `ArrayListUnmanaged = ArrayList` deprecated alias (std.zig:62), `smp_allocator` (heap.zig:351), posix `fork/close/write` gone while `read/poll/kill/tcsetattr/sigaction` remain (posix.zig:400/1003/378/1178/942), `Sigaction.handler fn(SIG) callconv(.c)` matches SignalManager.zig:51, pollfd `i16` (linux.zig:8094). | Fix: S.

### Explicitly clean (grepped src/ + tools/, zero hits)
`usingnamespace`, `std.process.argsAlloc`, `ArrayListUnmanaged`, manual `DebugAllocator`, `callconv(.C)`, `std.fs.cwd`, `fixedBufferStream`, `std.time.sleep`, `mem.indexOf`, `@cImport`, Tailwind-era `PROT.READ|...` (mmap at src/platform/linux/wayland.zig:727 uses modern packed structs). build.zig is fully current idiom: `createModule`+`root_module`, `strip` on Module, `lto` on Compile, `addPassthruArgs`. `init.gpa`/`smp_allocator` discipline is followed with written rationale at the two direct-use sites (src/persist/Session.zig:376, src/platform/windows/platform.zig:314).

### LOAD-BEARING — do not churn
The `--libc .cache/crt-fix-all/libc.txt` plumbing (still required, .sframe is a linker gap not a zig-version issue); `compat.zig`'s `MemWriter/forkExec/std.c` layer (retention rationale verified current — native Io.Writer has the APIs but the on-disk format stability argument stands); `compat.memsetU32` as the single revert point; `tools/zig-lib-fix.sh` (leave in place — it's the immune system for the next stdlib regression, costs nothing when std is clean).

---
**Summary:** Zig 0.17 alignment is in genuinely strong shape — the tree builds and passes its full test suite on today's stock dev.813 with zero deprecated-pattern hits, and the dreaded termios patched-lib-dir workaround has self-retired because upstream fixed the typo. The release risks in this area are packaging/docs, not code: a wrong "Zig 0.16+" requirement that will fail every source builder, a broken `make run-wm`, and an unlocked nix flake floating on zig master. All fixes are small (S); nothing here blocks the binaries you ship tomorrow, but the version-requirement doc fix should land before the announcement.

result.docs:
All docs read in full; ~15 claims spot-checked against code. Findings:

## Release-blocker triage findings

**BLOCKER | docs/AI-INTEGRATION.md:249-262 | In-band MCP section is false.** It claims "Tools exposed: all 57 tools" and shows `teru-query teruwm_list_windows`, `teru_broadcast`, `teruwm_scratchpad` working in-band. Code says the opposite: in-band is **opt-in** (`agent_in_band = true`, default OFF), narrow read-only allowlist, `teruwm_*` explicitly blocked, `pane_id` forced to caller's own pane (src/agent/in_band.zig:35-50; tests at :334-336 assert `!isToolAllowed("teruwm_list_windows")`). MCP-API.md:241-243 already states the correct behavior — the two docs directly contradict each other. The flagship "AI-first" doc promises a capability the code rejects; launch-day agents following it will fail. Fix: S-M.

**BLOCKER | README.md:15, docs/INSTALLING.md:68 | Wrong Zig version.** "Zig 0.16" / "Requires Zig 0.16+" vs build.zig.zon `minimum_zig_version = "0.17.0-dev"`. Every from-source builder (and the AUR package) on Zig 0.16 fails. Neither doc mentions the GCC-15 CRT `--libc` caveat or the zig-lib termios patch for `-Dcompositor` (CLAUDE.md documents both; user docs don't). State the known-good dev version + route users through `make`. Fix: S.

**HIGH | README.md:23-27, BENCHMARKS.md:74-80, ARCHITECTURE.md:317-325, INSTALLING.md:82-88 | Binary sizes stale by ~5x — in your favor.** All claim 6.6/5.6/11.9 MB. Actual stripped release artifacts (zig-out/bin, built 2026-06-10, strip now applied in build.zig): teru 1.41 MB, teruwm 1.22 MB, teruwmctl 0.37 MB. "A full Wayland compositor in 1.2 MB" is the launch headline currently sitting unclaimed. Re-measure with `make size`, update all four files. Fix: S.

**HIGH | tool-count drift across 5 files.** Actual: 22 teru + 38 teruwm = 60. README.md:30/43/139/152 says 59 (22+37); MCP-API.md:9 table says 37 but its own :304 heading says 38; AI-INTEGRATION.md:15 says 20, :45 says 20, :249 says 57; anchors `#teru-terminal-mcp--20-tools` (AI-INTEGRATION:71) and `#teruwm-compositor-mcp--24-tools` (:89) and ARCHITECTURE's `#teruwm-compositor-mcp--37-tools` (:275) are all broken links. Pick "60 tools (22 + 38)" once, fix anchors. Fix: S.

**HIGH | README.md (whole file) + docs/assets/ (empty) | Zero screenshots.** A compositor — a visual product — launches tomorrow with a presentation and not one image or GIF anywhere in the repo docs. First 10 lines have what/why/install but no proof-of-life. One hero screenshot + a 20-30s GIF (agent driving panes over MCP) is worth more than any prose fix in this report. Fix: M (asset capture, you have native `$mod+W` + wf-recorder for this).

The env var is exported (src/modes/windowed.zig:367), but there is no public Claude Code contract named `CLAUDE_PANE_BACKEND_SOCKET` I can verify; if this needs a fork/unreleased client, the no-config claim fails for every user on day one. ## Per-file editorial plan

- **README.md (173)** — right length; wrong emphasis. Lead with the author's actual story: "a lightweight, AI-first Wayland compositor (and terminal) in Zig" + screenshot above the fold; the negative-space tagline ("No GPU, no FreeType...") should follow, not lead. Add a 4-bullet "ecosystem" line (native screenshots/area-select, recording, scratchpads, launcher, session persistence, notifications-in-bar) — all shipped, all currently buried in KEYBINDINGS/CONFIGURATION. Cut nothing else; fix sizes/counts/Zig version.
- **AI-INTEGRATION.md (358)** — the launch centerpiece; needs an accuracy pass (in-band scope, CustomPaneBackend, tool counts, mcp.json path), not restructuring. The reboot-survivable-agent section is genuinely strong — keep.
- **MCP-API.md (528)** — canonical and mostly correct; fix 37/38 self-contradiction, /tmp path, dead anchors. Fine at this length for a reference.
- **CONFIGURATION.md (766)** — only doc over budget. Split at line 457 into teru.conf and teruwm-config docs; the current merge forces a confusing "which options are inert where" paragraph (:459-461) that a split dissolves. M.
- **INSTALLING.md (258)** — lines 142-222 (startt/dev-install/recompile-restart inner loop) are contributor docs, not install docs; move to HACKING.md/CONTRIBUTING and the file becomes a tight 150 lines. M.
- **KEYBINDINGS.md (434)** — fix broken table; otherwise the best reference doc here.
- **BENCHMARKS.md (198)** — best-written doc in the repo; the "what we do NOT measure" stance is a differentiator. Just re-run numbers.
- **ARCHITECTURE.md (348), DEBUGGING.md (135), SESSIONS.md (190)** — healthy; DEBUGGING's "improvement plan" (:111-135) is an internal roadmap, trim to an issue link; SESSIONS is the only doc with Gnosis frontmatter (style inconsistency, harmless).
- **Tone overall** — far less LLM-ish than typical: opinionated, first-person, honest ("Publishing one anyway would be marketing, not measurement"). De-robotify targets: bold-prefix bullet rash in README "Why", absolutes ("no other..."), version parentheticals, and self-labels like "Honest numbers" (show, don't self-certify).
- **Missing collateral** — no FAQ, no comparison table (vs sway/hyprland+tmux, alacritty/kitty+tmux — the BENCHMARKS peer table is the seed), no 5-minute demo script for the presentation, no "first 10 minutes" quickstart that ends in an agent driving a pane. One new `docs/COMPARED.md` + a demo script covers it. M.

## Top 10 changes, ranked by launch impact

1. Fix AI-INTEGRATION in-band section to match src/agent/in_band.zig (opt-in, allowlist, no teruwm_*) — BLOCKER, S-M
2. Fix Zig version (0.17.0-dev) + GCC-15/zig-lib build caveats in README + INSTALLING — BLOCKER, S
3. Add hero screenshot + demo GIF to README (docs/assets is empty) — HIGH, M
4. Re-measure binaries; replace 6.6/5.6/11.9 MB with ~1.4/1.2/0.4 MB in 4 files — new headline — HIGH, S
5. Single-source tool count (22+38=60); fix all broken anchors — HIGH, S
6. Verify-or-soften CLAUDE_PANE_BACKEND_SOCKET claim; unify the mcp.json snippet across both docs — HIGH, S
7. Rewrite README top 10 lines: AI-first compositor story + ecosystem bullets; soften "no other has" — MED, S-M
8. Fix KEYBINDINGS broken table + grave-chord escaping — MED, S
9. Purge personal paths (~/.miozu, ~/code/workbench, /tmp suite) from 4 docs — MED, S
10. Strip v0.4.x version stamps; split CONFIGURATION at the teruwm boundary; move INSTALLING dev-loop to HACKING — MED, M

Summary:
Docs are unusually honest and human for this genre — BENCHMARKS/SESSIONS are launch-ready as-is — but accuracy has drifted hard behind a fast-moving codebase: two flat-out false claims (in-band MCP scope, Zig 0.16) would burn launch-day users, and tool counts/test counts/binary sizes disagree across five files. The single biggest miss is visual: zero screenshots of a visual product, plus the 1.2 MB binary-size headline going unclaimed. One focused day fixes items 1-9; only the CONFIGURATION split and demo assets are real work.
